title: KRUPS Precision Flat Coffee Burr Grinder, 12-Grind fineness settings, Black
description:
About this item
Versatile Grinding: 12 precision settings from fine to coarse, perfect for various brewing methods including drip coffee, french press, espresso, pour over, and cold brew; customize your grind to extract optimal flavor from your coffee beans
Precision Burr Mechanism: Flat metallic burrs ensure uniform grinding for consistent flavor extraction; the powerful 110-watt motor maintains optimal grinding speed for preserving coffee's aromatic qualities
Large Capacity: 8 oz bean hopper and removable grounds container can produce enough coffee for up to 32 cups; ideal for both single servings and larger batches to accommodate various brewing needs; efficient design minimizes countertop space
User-Friendly Operation: Features an intuitive on/off button with auto-stop functionality; select the number of cups (2-12) for precise grinding; removable top burr and included cleaning brush ensure easy maintenance
Premium Construction: Crafted with durable stainless steel for longevity and consistent performance; sleek design complements modern kitchen aesthetics while delivering professional-grade grinding results at home
reviews_5s:
I never thought I’d fall in love with an appliance, but here we are. The KRUPS Burr Grinder has become the MVP of my mornings, turning my groggy pre-coffee self into a caffeinated champion. It grinds like a pro, ensuring that my coffee is fresh, flavorful, and just the right consistency every single time. Honestly, it’s like having a barista in my kitchen—minus the man bun and ironic tattoos.

Setup is a breeze. The settings are easy to adjust, whether you’re brewing a robust espresso or a smooth French press. It’s versatile, consistent, and, most importantly, quieter than my old grinder, which sounded like a jet engine warming up for takeoff. My neighbors are probably as grateful as I am.

What I love most is how simple it is to clean. No more battling rogue coffee grounds that seem to appear out of thin air. KRUPS keeps the mess contained and my counters crumb-free, which is a small miracle in itself.

If you’re a coffee enthusiast—or just someone who needs caffeine to humanize in the morning—this grinder is worth every penny. It’s dependable, efficient, and dare I say… grind-tastic. KRUPS has definitely upped my coffee game, and for that, I salute it (with a steaming mug of freshly ground coffee, of course).

Bottom line: Buy it. Your taste buds (and sanity) will thank you. Cheers to fresh mornings and even fresher coffee! ☕✨Purchased this burr grinder after blade grinder died. I wanted a grinder with a variety of settings, to prepare espresso and drip coffees and one that seemed to have enough power and strength to do the job and last. This KRUPS grinds coffee beans accurately, from coarse to very fine based on your setting and does the job quickly. I like that it is shorter than some burr grinders and fits underneath my kitchen cabinets. The burrs themselves appear strong and secure. The receptacles for the coffee beans and coffee grinds are easy to install and remove and seat themselves securely. Durability is yet to be determined but it appears well made. Happy with this purchase.This is, without any doubt or equivocation, the finest grinder that I have ever owned. It is certainly a tremendous value especially at the sale price at which I purchased the item.

It is noisy! However, I would be noisy too if the first thing that I had to do each morning was to fill my mouth with coffee beans and then, with my teeth, grind up enough for a couple of pots of coffee.

Trust me on this, I would be howling, and growling, and barking out a LITANY of profane epithets, unceasingly!!

There are settings for volume of bean to be ground in order to meet the liquid demands, and enough settings to grind the beans precisely as I like them. This setting, obviously, will be determined by the coffee blend as well as the apparatus that is employed to tackle this task. Along with the preferential whims of the human behind the magic!! Quality coffee brewing is NOT a task for the faint at heart. Their very state of emotional and physical wellness may depend upon their level competence, passion, and level of expertise, that are mandated by the task of conjuring up that precious morning commodity :)

The machine has a very large bay into which I poor the appropriate amount of product, being careful not to overfill beyond my immediate need. The machine has an exceptionally large bean capacity. I use one heaping coffee measuring scoop per cup of coffee. Subsequently, I fill the bean bay up 2/3 of the way. This is the maximum amount of grounds that the coffee ground catch basket will hold. Furthermore, the filters won't accommodate beyond that ... at all!!!

I no longer subscribe to the old men's tales that the consumption of a combination of slices of hockey puck look'n toast, dipped into a 12 oz. cauldron of spoon dissolving coffee, that has the same color, flavor, and texture as the aforementioned toast, will put hair on my chest.

Lastly, the negligible amount of finely ground coffee that escapes the clutches of the grounds receptacle is hardly worth mentioning. I utilize the grinder as it sits on either a dish rag, or dish towel. This completely alleviates any concerns of coffee dust on my counters. It also absorbs all of the vibration of the grinder on the counter, which effectively lowers the decibel level of the grinding process.

As well designed and crafted as this grinder is, again, I genuinely believe that it is going to outlast me!! Hence, it will be welcome an appropriate comrade to accompany the KOFFEE tamper that I use to PACK the grounds into the filter.

The end result of this entire process is DARK STRONG, BLACK, HOT KOFFEE!!

Yep, and it all starts with, and depends upon, my trusty Krups Precision Flat Burr Grinder :)This Krups coffee grinder does exactly what it is supposed to do and I love it. I've had burr grinders before and have had the Krups spice grinder for years. When my last coffee grinder failed I just used the Krups spice grinder which does a great job on spices but not as great for coffee. I didn't want the same brand of coffee grinder that failed before so purchased this one and I'm glad I did. I am not a high-end connoisseur of coffee but I know what I like and and I know what I want. There are various recommended grind settings for this unit that I've tried: the French Press [wonderful], the AeroPress [perfect], and drip coffee [just right]. You can tweak it up or down from that recommendation as your taste buds dictate. The number of cups selector works just fine and is accurate. If you weigh coffee by the gram, then the amount might not be exact but for your preferred brew method. I use a scale. Plus, our coffee is only as good as our beans.

I see people have mentioned the coffee "dust" this creates when grinding. All types of grinders create dust. When you remove the container there will be dust against the side of it thanks to static which is everywhere.

Cleaning tip - use canned air. Unplug and remove the upper burr [set grind to finest and rotate burr to the right then lift up]. Use the cleaning brush on both the upper and lower burr, then blow out any remaining dust and small particles with canned air. Be sure to clean out the little "dispenser" port to the container. Set grind level to coarse, put the upper burr back in and rotate to the left. I use mine every day so take it apart about every week to ten days. It does make a difference!This grinder works great, such an improvement on my small blade grinder. While it produces a small amount of fines, the rest appears quite consistent in size. I use an Aeropress and use grind #4. Using this grinder made a dramatic improvement in the taste of the coffee. Some reviewers complain about the inconsistency of the "number of cups" to grind, but if you are sincere about wanting a great cuppa joe you are probably going to measure anyway so I don't see that as a big deal. The noise level is well below that of my old blade grinder. Perhaps spending another 80 or 100 dollars on a higher end machine would result in even further improvement but that's hard to imagine. This little machine does what I need at a good price.I had purchased a Cuisinart burr grinder for the hubs that was in his wishlist. We were disappointed in it and returned it.

Back to the drawing board. After looking at lots of burr grinders we settled on the Krups. We'd always had great luck with the Krups inexpensive blade grinder, so decided to stick with the brand. Ordered in the morning as he received it that night!

We LOVE it! Works perfectly on the medium (#6) setting for drip coffee and also in the coarsest setting for my cold brew.

For the reasonable price, this appears to fit the bill for us. If it proves to be as durable as our $20 Krups blade grinder, we will be more than satisfied.I like the improved flavor of fresh ground coffee beans. I wanted to mark the grinder down to four stars, because I have yet to get the ground coffee to the coffee maker from the grinder without making a mess. At first I blamed the grinder. But, I am starting to think I am just a clutz.

I like the fact I can set the grind and the amount separately. It is also a burr grinder, which I think it better.
The one downside, other than the mess I make, is the amount of "fines." I don't consider that a major problem unless I am using a French press. I will keep trying, maybe one morning I won't end up with ground coffee on the counter.I’ve been using a regular grinder for many years but wanted a grinder with more options. Reviews on this one commented about “spillage” but it’s not a deal breaker especially if you’re careful. Some commented it was noisy. No worse than regular coffee grinders in fact it’s not as loud. I love the different options for grind size. I actually use slightly less beans than is suggested to make 8 cups in my Cuisinart drip machine. I like a good strong flavor but not overly strong. When pouring out the grounds into the basket you have to use a spoon otherwise if you tap it against the basket yes you’ll get coffee grounds everywhere.My husband loves his new coffee grinder! It grinds well. Not as loud as others we've had. Lightweight. Buy this. You won't be sorry.This Coffee Grinder is a fantastic choice for anyone serious about their coffee. This grinder stands out for its precision, build quality, and user-friendly design.

What sets this grinder apart is its consistent grind quality. Whether you’re brewing espresso, French press, or pour-over, the burr mechanism ensures uniform grind size, which is essential for flavor extraction. The grind settings are highly adjustable, ranging from ultra-fine to coarse, with clear markers for precision.

Would buy it again..
reviews_1s:
Bought my Krupps coffee grinder in Feb ‘24…grinds just enough for daily use. Today it filled up the pot with ground coffee. The measured grind is not working properly.

It has 2 year warranty. I called Krupps. We will send you a label where you can return to our repair center and we’ll try to fix it…if we can’t fix it, we’ll replace it. Now that sounds fair enough…but let’s look at it realistically. I need to box it and take it to ups. Week to get to repair center? Goes on shelf waiting for its turn…he said “several weeks”?? Anybody buying that? Then they repair/replace? Estimated turnaround from ground coffee at my house to ground coffee again? My guess? 1 1/2-2 months. Do I just buy another group under while I wait?? Their website also offers to send me parts so I can watch a video and fix it myself??

All this on a $30-$40 purchase? I wish it was still covered by Amazon returns. Worked great for about 4 months. Not great. Warranty is a joke. My guess is most people just chalk it up to bad product and not mess with the repair hassle. Which probably is a large factor in Krupps repair policy decisions.I love the grind of this coffee grinder - it's just perfect. I bought this a couple of years ago but only recently took it out. It only lasted a year, so if I buy it again it will definitely be with the replacement warranty. The other thing that's hard for me is the width of the ground coffee container - I can't grip it with just one hand so it is awkward. Nevertheless, I would have used this one forever because of the perfect grind. Really sad.Maybe Krups will be kind to me.I initially purchased the grinder in February 2024, and it worked well for cold brew coffee on the coarse grind setting. However, it is now October 2024, and the machine would not even turn on. I tried the common troubleshooting steps provided in the manual -- checking that the output is functional, the hopper lid is firmly closed, the coarse ground container is properly inserted, and the burr grinder is connected. However, the machine is completely unresponsive.

I have contacted KRUPS customer support, and will update if I hear a response.

EDIT: It has been nearly two months. After my initial contact with customer support, I was told to submit a ticket with pictures. I immediately did this but then --- radio silence. I have heard absolutely nothing from them. Don't order from KRUPS if you want product support down the line!The coffee grinder worked sporadically at first. Then stopped operating completely within a month. Krups customer service website only offers a repair option not a return although on Amazon it states returns are accepted within a certain timeframe, . Krups website did not accept my repair request. The data input fields blocked input making it very difficult to write a repair request. Then the website would not accept the written request.Set it to the coarsest setting for French press and you get coarse, medium, fine, and dust so fine it static-clings.
Set it to medium and you get medium, fine, and more dust. Etc.

The reason for upgrading from a blade grinder to a burr grinder is better consistency of grind, but this is worse.I thought the Krups Brand Name would provide me with a excellent product. However, every time I use this Grinder, I wind up having to clean my counter of a lot ofcoffee grounds that have shot out of this Krups Burr Grinder - Not one of my better buys of trusting a brand name.Ok. This thing grinds coffee quite well. But everything else about this damned thing absolutely SUCKS. 1-Supposedly will grind the proper amount of coffee for 2-12 cups. WRONG. Lowest amount is about 5 cups, or full size french press. To use my personal french press, I have to grind the coffee, put it in another container, then measure it-I was really excited to be bale to grind the exact amount, but it DOES NOT DO THAT, AS ADVERTISED. 2-The hopper for the grounds is a weird shape, so pouring coffee out of it totally ensures you will spill it everywhere. 3-Not sure why, but this thing generates static like no tomorrow! Pouring coffee can be a lovely science project, as the coffee will quite literally travel sideways to stick to the outside of the hopper, or anything on the counter, rather than pour into whatever you actually want the grounds in. It makes a mess of my counters and floor every time I use it, to the point that I actually started buy ground coffee-something I haven't done in 25 or more years. This thing aggravates the living hell out of me every time I use it, and it's a crappy way to start your day....EVERY DAY. Don't buy it! I expected MUCH more from Krupps, and they have lost a customer for life. Inexcusable to sell a product with these flaws.First off, this is a massive grinder. The rather albatross grinder will consume so much space on your counter such that all who visit you will know you grind your coffee fresh every morning. The hopper is rather generous and will accommodate almost a small bag of beans. Good thing too because based on the footprint of the grinder you won't want to also have beans stored at the ready. The grind takes some time to hone in. Don't bother adjusting though. Because for some reason a 6 on the arbitrary grind scale is never the same 6 twice. While we're at it might as well mention the cups of coffee selection is ridiculous. Wicked inconsistent when grinding as well... look forward to a mixture of superfine to course in all settings (just what that balance is can be adjusted out with the inaccurate settings). This being said..... just don't bother with expresso. Makes a mess all around it for some reason as well. Just all in all sloppy. It was cheap and on sale or it would have gone back. Good enough for drip coffee if you aren't picky. But rest assured the performance will not match the product description.This coffee bean grinder is not good… I have spent so much money trying different beans but its really just the grinder. It claims it has different grind settings and they all come out the same no matter the option. I have wasted so much coffee because it doesnt come out right. And i cant even return because i dont have the box anymore, i would not recommend purchasingMy wife set the grinder to make 12 cups and it made double that. And coffee grounds were everywhere. The instructions are minimal to non existent. I gave this to my wife for Christmas. She used it once and she’s sending it back.
